# Practice File Attribution Template

When creating new practice files, use this template to ensure proper attribution:

```markdown
# Practice Title

**Concept Source**: Based on practices from [rails-bestpractices.com](link-to-article)

**Original Article Date**: Date from article

**Original Author**: Author name (if known)

**Note**: This practice is rewritten in our own words based on the concept. 
The practice itself is a fact about Rails behavior and community knowledge.

## Description

[Your own description in your own words - do NOT copy verbatim from the website]

## Why

[Your own explanation - do NOT copy verbatim]

## Bad Example

[Your own example or original example]

## Good Example

[Your own example or original example]

## Related Practices

[Links to related practices]

## Tags

[Relevant tags]
```

## Key Points

1. **Always Attribute**: Include source link and original author
2. **Use Your Own Words**: Never copy verbatim text
3. **Note the Approach**: State that content is rewritten
4. **Clarify Nature**: Note that practices are facts/community knowledge
5. **Add Value**: Include your own examples and explanations
